question_text: Which statement about magnetic poles is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Like poles repel each other and unlike poles repel each other

options.label: B
options.text: Like poles attract each other and unlike poles repel each other

options.label: C
options.text: Like poles repel each other and unlike poles attract each other

options.label: D
options.text: Both like and unlike poles always attract each other

explanation: Like poles push each other away (repel), so north-north and south-south repel. Unlike poles pull towards each other (attract), so north-south attract. This is a key rule in magnetism.
